title: salvia majdae
intro: Salvia majdae, also known as Khaysonj or Sonjbolouk, is a plant species native to Iran and Afghanistan. This perennial herb belongs to the mint family and is used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Salvia majdae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms such as indigestion, bloating, and respiratory issues.
